name: Monforte de Lemos - Loop along the
name_original: Monforte de Lemos – vuelta por río Cabe
description: Enjoy an easy 14.9-mile touring cycling route along the scenic Cabe River in Monforte de Lemos, perfect for all skill levels.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Monforte De Lemos are set within the diverse landscapes of the Ribeira Sacra region, characterized by deep river canyons, terraced vineyards, and rolling hills. The area features an extensive network of routes, including dedicated paths and adapted trails. Elevations vary from gentle riverside paths along the Cabe River to challenging climbs with significant gradients, particularly within the Sil River Canyon. Best touring cycling routes around Monforte De Lemos The most popular touring cycling route is Monforte de Lemos - return by Cabe river, an easy 14.9 miles (23.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant ride along the Cabe River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Amandi – Roman Granary of Proendos loop from Monforte de Lemos, a difficult 28.4 miles (45.7 km) path that winds through the Ribeira Sacra e Serras do Oribio e do Courel Biosphere Reserve. Local touring cyclists also love the Sil River Canyon – View of the Sil River Valley loop from Monforte de Lemos, a 66.7 miles (107.4 km) trail leading through dramatic river canyons and terraced vineyards, often completed in about 7 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Monforte De Lemos is defined by river valleys, historic vineyards, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Monforte De Lemos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Monforte De Lemos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Monforte de Lemos?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Monforte de Lemos, offering a wide range of experiences from easy riverside paths to challenging climbs through the Ribeira Sacra region.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017336517/monforte-de-lemos-return-by-cabe-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monforte de Lemos - return by Cabe river&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 23.9 km path that offers a pleasant ride along the Cabe River.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Monforte de Lemos?
A: The terrain is highly varied, reflecting the diverse landscapes of the Ribeira Sacra. You'll find everything from gentle, paved paths along rivers to challenging, unpaved segments with significant gradients, especially in areas like the Sil River Canyon. Routes often combine roads, asphalted tracks, and natural trails.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5328524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amandi – Roman Granary of Proendos loop from Monforte de Lemos&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 45.7 km route that winds through the Ribeira Sacra e Serras do Oribio e do Courel Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the dramatic Sil River Canyon, enjoy views from Mount San Vicente, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5016863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other stunning viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcones de Madrid Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the landscape and terraced vineyards.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Monforte de Lemos and its surroundings are steeped in history. You can explore the Monastery of San Vicente do Pino and its Castle, the impressive Colegio Nuestra Señora de la Antigua, or the ancient Monastery of Santa Cristina. The historic Old Quarter of Monforte de Lemos and the medieval Ponte Vella also offer cultural insights.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Monforte de Lemos?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. Spring offers lush greenery and comfortable temperatures, while autumn provides stunning fall colors, especially in the vineyards. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are often preferred for longer rides.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Monforte de Lemos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Monforte de Lemos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the climbs, and the beauty of the river canyons and vineyards.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the famous Sil River Canyon?
A: Yes, the Sil River Canyon is a highlight of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sil River Canyon – View of the Sil River Valley loop from Monforte de Lemos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 107.4 km route that takes you through dramatic river canyons and past terraced vineyards, offering breathtaking views.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling trails?
A: Monforte de Lemos and the surrounding towns in Ribeira Sacra offer various cafes, restaurants, and accommodations. Many routes pass through or near villages where you can find places to refuel or stay overnight, especially in Monforte de Lemos itself, which serves as an excellent base.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty routes for touring cyclists?
A: Yes, there are several moderate routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Direction to Martín – Restaurante Capitol loop from Monforte de Lemos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.4 km route that provides a good balance of challenge and enjoyment without being overly strenuous.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on the more challenging routes?
A: The more challenging routes in Monforte de Lemos can feature significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sil River Canyon – View of the Sil River Valley loop from Monforte de Lemos&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of nearly 2,300 meters, making for a demanding but rewarding ride.
tips: Ponte vella, (old bridge in Galician) is a masonry arch bridge that crosses the Cabo River. The origin of the bridge is believed to date back to Roamn times, but the current bridge dates back to the last decade of the 16th century.

A defensive fortress that dominates the city and the surrounding landscape, the castle of Monforte inspires respect. Climb up to enjoy the views. Built between the 13th and 15th centuries, the towers still stand. In the same place you can see the Monastery and Manor House of the Counts of Lemos, part of which was converted into a hotel.

Wooded recreation area on both sides of the Cabe River. In a landscape surrounded by water and native trees declared a Special Protection Area, integrated into the Natura 2000 Network. The river passes through Monforte de Lemos, the capital of the Ribeira Sacra natural park.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Monforte de Lemos - return by Cabe river route?
faqs.answer: This easy touring bicycle route typically takes around 1 hour and 30 minutes to complete. The total distance is approximately 23.9 kilometers (14.85 miles).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is! The route is categorized as an "easy bike ride" and is suitable for all fitness levels. Its predominantly paved surface and modest elevation gain make it ideal for beginners and those seeking a relaxed experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly paved, ensuring a smooth ride for touring bicycles. You can expect a comfortable journey with very few unpaved sections, making it accessible and enjoyable.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Monforte de Lemos - return by Cabe river route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views along the Cabe River. Notable landmarks include the historic Ponte Vella de Monforte de Lemos, the impressive Monforte de Lemos Castle, and the Cabe River Dam. The river itself provides a tranquil and visually appealing backdrop throughout the journey.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable year-round. Autumn offers vibrant colors, especially along the riverside, while winter can present frosty, scenic views. Spring and summer provide pleasant weather for cycling, making it a versatile option for any season.

faqs.question: Is the Monforte de Lemos - return by Cabe river route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route follows public paths, specific regulations for dogs on bicycles can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near other cyclists and pedestrians. Always ensure your dog is comfortable and safe if riding with you.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Monforte de Lemos - return by Cabe river route. It uses public access paths along the river.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route in Monforte de Lemos?
faqs.answer: Monforte de Lemos offers various parking options within the town center, close to the Cabe River. Look for public parking areas near the riverbanks, which will provide convenient access to the starting points of the route.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants along the Cabe River cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route integrates with Monforte de Lemos, you'll find various amenities. There are cafes, pubs, and restaurants available within the city center and along the river's edge, perfect for a break or a meal.

faqs.question: Is bike rental available in Monforte de Lemos?
faqs.answer: Yes, bike rental services are typically available in Monforte de Lemos. It's recommended to check with local tourism offices or sports shops in advance to arrange for a suitable touring bicycle.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other local paths. You'll find it briefly passes through parts of the Camiño de Inverno, Rúa dos Centeás, and Camiño da Barxa, among others, offering a glimpse into the wider network of trails in the area.
